commit.message:
Release WebGate FW3.7

New features:
- SSH backdoor via RCE CVE with Scope Agreement (typed 'I accept')
- TOR routing via bridge (obfs4/webtunnel/snowflake)
- SQLMap interactive dump dialog: pick DB -> table -> dump in GUI
- CVEExploitOfferDialog: auto-prompt when RCE CVE detected after scan
- ScopeAgreementDialog: typed 'I accept' confirmation before exploit
- SSHBackdoorDialog: configure SSH credentials + TOR bridge
- SQLMapDumpDialog: GUI-based DB/table selector with background dump

Improvements:
- Simplified reports: SQL injection Y/N + vulnerable param/payload
- XSStrike output simplified to injection Y/N + vulnerable URL
- Progress bar with gradient, % overlay, bright pulse edge animation
- Logo glitch animation (random interval glitch effect)
- Particle burst animation on scan start
- FW badge in logo canvas
- Scan-line border animation during active scan

Fixes:
- Removed COPY button (caused GUI freeze/lag on Wayland)
- Version strings updated to FW3.7
- LICENSE added (authorized use only)
- README.md fully rewritten with all features documented

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
debug: expose customFields in GHL contacts response

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Skip lead_time=0h for ECMWF max/min step_type variables (#492)

* Fix KeyError for wind_gust_10m at lead_time=0h in ECMWF IFS ENS region job

10fg (wind gust) is a max-window variable absent from the GRIB index at
lead_time=0h since there is no prior post-processing step at initialization
time. The operational update was generating source file coords for all lead
times including 0h, causing a KeyError when looking up '10fg' in the index.

Fix by adding has_hour_0_values() to ecmwf_utils following the same pattern
as noaa_utils, and filtering lead_time=0h coords in generate_source_file_coords
for any variable group where not all variables have hour 0 values.

Update the slow integration test to use a dynamic recent init_time (instead of
hardcoded 2026-01-01) and test via generate_source_file_coords over lead_times
[0h, 3h] so it would catch this class of bug in the future.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01LBZoe7ccmmgZxdKD8uHhZr

* Parallelize slow test downloads with ThreadPoolExecutor

https://claude.ai/code/session_01LBZoe7ccmmgZxdKD8uHhZr

* Group source_groups by has_hour_0_values; add ecmwf_utils unit tests

- source_groups now groups by (date_available, has_hour_0_values) so all
  variables in a group share the same hour-0 behaviour, allowing
  generate_source_file_coords to use item() for a clean single-value check
- Add tests/ecmwf/test_ecmwf_utils.py with parametrized unit tests for
  has_hour_0_values covering all step_types and the override mechanism

https://claude.ai/code/session_01LBZoe7ccmmgZxdKD8uHhZr

* Hoist group_has_hour_0_values out of the init_time/lead_time loop

https://claude.ai/code/session_01LBZoe7ccmmgZxdKD8uHhZr

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update sort dropdown UI immediately on sort/order change

The sort dropdown button label and item highlights are outside
#artist-content, so HTMX outerHTML swaps never update them. Add
updateSortUI() to patch the DOM immediately when setSort/setOrder
are called, keeping the toolbar in sync without a full page reload.

Also address Copilot review feedback:
- Assert artistSvc.Create errors in test setup
- Tighten sort/order assertions to target the specific hidden inputs
  (artist-sort-input, artist-order-input) and verify both sort and order

commit.message:
Fix AI context silent failures + add configurable max tokens

- Remove .is("date_deleted", null) from app_contracts and app_content
  view queries in fetchClientContext and fetchWorkspaceSummary — these
  views don't have a date_deleted column, causing silent query failures
  that returned zero contracts/content for ALL clients
- Also remove it from app_clients in fetchWorkspaceSummary
- Add aiMaxTokens workspace setting: API GET/PATCH, messages route
  passes to streaming, settings UI with 2K/4K/8K/16K selector

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(logout): clear lastSyncHlc so re-login triggers full pull

After logout the Room DB is not cleared, so hasLocalData=true and
the stored HLC cursor was reused on re-login. Documents created on
other devices while Android was offline (or with an HLC older than
the stored cursor) were silently skipped.

Clearing lastSyncHlc on logout forces since="0" on the next pull,
which (combined with the backend fix in the previous commit) now
returns ALL documents regardless of device_id.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Phase 2.9: Wire Full Pipeline & Update CLAUDE.md docs (#5)

* Update CLAUDE.md files to reflect Phase 2 implementation progress

Sync all component CLAUDE.md files with phases 2.1–2.9 implementation:
- Root: update status, topology, ports, service list, repos volume docs
- go-services: add RepoSyncer, update PRReview v2 pipeline, branch index
- reviewer: add tools (read_file, search_codebase), ReviewDeps, tests
- api-server: add migration 000008_branch_indexes
- search-mcp: fix stale indexer/main.py reference
- e2e: add full test case table (18 tests)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Phase 2.9: Wire Full Pipeline (PRReview.Run v2)

Wire the complete Phase 2 pipeline: debounce → fetch → dedup → draft guard
→ SyncRepo → IndexRepo → Reviewer (with tools) → PostReview.

- PRReview.Run now calls RepoSyncer.SyncRepo and Indexer.IndexRepo
- Pass repo_path, target_branch_sha, search_collection to Reviewer
- Add GetBranchIndex/UpsertBranchIndex for index state tracking
- Skip indexing when branch already at head_sha
- SyncRepo failure is fatal; IndexRepo failure is non-fatal (graceful degradation)
- Add 6 new e2e tests: TestSemanticSearch, TestRepoSyncerCloneFailure,
  TestIndexerFailureGracefulDegradation, TestReadFileToolWorksWithSyncedRepo, etc.
- Update mock servers to support git repo creation and indexer responses

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add 500 hPa geopotential height variable to GEFS datasets (#490)

* Add 500 hPa geopotential height to GEFS analysis and forecast datasets

https://claude.ai/code/session_01LBd3sDwYwxRihWA2hLLnPj

* Add integration tests for geopotential_height_500hpa download and read across all GEFS data periods

https://claude.ai/code/session_01LBd3sDwYwxRihWA2hLLnPj

* Add geopotential_height_500hpa to GEFS analysis and forecast datasets

- Add geopotential_height_500hpa data variable to shared GEFS template config
  with gefs_file_type="a" (a-files only; 500 hPa not in s-files), correct
  grib_description using Pascal units ('50000[Pa] ISBL="Isobaric surface"'),
  and index_position=31
- Add "500 mb": "pres_abv700mb" to GEFS_REFORECAST_LEVELS_SHORT so reforecast
  URLs resolve to the hgt_pres_abv700mb_* files (500 hPa is not in hgt_pres_*)
- Regenerate zarr templates for both datasets
- Replace single-variable slow tests with 4 comprehensive all-vars integration
  tests per dataset: reforecast, pre-v12, current early lead, current later
  lead (forecast only); uses source_groups() with full groups per test,
  skipping vars absent from the reforecast archive via FileNotFoundError

https://claude.ai/code/session_01LBd3sDwYwxRihWA2hLLnPj

* Fix slow tests to handle vars missing from older GEFS periods

Some variables aren't present in pre-v12 or reforecast GRIB files (e.g.
cloud ceiling HGT, PRMSL). Catch FileNotFoundError, ValueError, and
AssertionError to skip missing vars rather than failing the test.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01LBd3sDwYwxRihWA2hLLnPj

* Fix reforecast 0.5deg files and add explicit missing-var allow-lists

read_rasterio: reforecast pressure-level files (e.g. hgt_pres_abv700mb)
are 0.5deg while surface files are 0.25deg. When the reforecast file
resolution doesn't match the output grid, reproject it the same way as
a/b-files instead of asserting equal shape. This fixes geopotential_
height_500hpa in the GEFSv12 reforecast period (2000-2019).

Tests: replace broad exception catches with explicit frozenset allow-lists
(_REFORECAST_MISSING_VARS, _PRE_V12_MISSING_VARS) so unexpected failures
are caught rather than silently skipped.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01LBd3sDwYwxRihWA2hLLnPj

* Address PR review comments on GEFS geopotential height PR

- DRY read_data.py: merge "a"|"b"|"reforecast" into one case with a
  single reproject block; separate "s" case with assert reader.shape
  == out_spatial_shape restored
- Remove reforecast and pre-v12 forecast download/read tests (dataset
  starts 2020-10-01 with GEFSv12)
- Consolidate current early/later lead forecast tests into one
  parametrized test with ThreadPoolExecutor over source groups
- Add ThreadPoolExecutor parallelism to all analysis download/read tests

fix: Use current month when promoting RC from previous month

When promoting an RC to stable, the workflow now checks if the RC is
from a previous month. If so, it starts fresh with the current month
instead of keeping the old month's version number.

Before: Feb RC v2026.2.8-rc.1 promoted to v2026.2.8 in March
After: Feb RC triggers fresh v2026.3.1 when promoted in March

fix: кнопка приглашения + диалог настроек уведомлений + чекбокс оплаты

- employees_tab: кнопка приглашения теперь всегда видима, 3 состояния:
  серая (нет email/прав) → синяя (активна) → зелёная user-check (Telegram подключён)

commit.message:
feat: Pricing Action column + price↔spread cross-driving

Pricing Action column (before Price in Applied group):
- New pricingAction field on Inquiry (string | null, null until APPLY)
- Human-readable label: "TW bid +0.50c", "CP+ offer -10.0bp", "CBBT mid"
  Fixed-income convention: 'ask' displayed as 'offer'
- Coloured by direction: positive markup = #00c896 (green),
  negative markup = #ff7f5c (orange-red), neutral = #7ab3cc (steel blue)
- Column positioned first in the Applied group, width 140px, monospace font

Price ↔ Spread cross-driving (duration-based approximation):
- priceUtils.ts: estimateModDuration(coupon, maturity) uses the
  Macaulay-at-par formula with annual compounding; estimateDV01 derives
  price-points-per-bp from modified duration × mid price / 10 000
- computeApplied() now always returns BOTH price AND spread:
    units='c': price = ref_side_price + markup
               spread = ref_mid_spread − (price − ref_mid_price) / DV01
    units='bp': spread = ref_side_spread + markup
                price  = ref_mid_price  − (spread − ref_mid_spread) × DV01
- PriceSimulator re-derives both price and spread on every 400 ms tick
  so both cells follow the live reference data continuously
- App.tsx handleApply writes pricingAction + both price + spread; the
  appliedConfig stored on the row keeps the simulator in sync

Example cross-drive (UST 4.25% 2034, D_mod≈6.8yr, DV01≈0.068):
  markup −5bp on TW mid → spread = mid_spread − 5, price tighter by ~0.34pts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add performance monitoring to build_features

Task 1 — [PERF] step timing:
  fetch_prices / fetch_institutional / fetch_margin_short /
  fetch_adj_factor / fetch_stock_master / fetch_fundamental /
  merge_fundamental / fetch_theme_flow / compute_features / save_features
  Format: [PERF] {step}: {elapsed:.2f}s（{n_stocks}檔 × {n_days}天）

Task 2 — tqdm progress bar:
  tqdm over ProcessPoolExecutor chunks, desc="建立特徵 (N檔 × D天)"

Task 3 — psutil resource monitoring:
  log_system_resources(stage) at run() start, before/after compute, end
  Format: [RESOURCE] {stage} | CPU: X% | RAM: X.XGB / X.XGB

Task 4 — per-feature timing → artifacts/feature_perf.csv:
  Per-feature elapsed time accumulated across all stocks
  Columns: feature, total_ms, n_stocks, n_days, avg_ms_per_stock
  Sorted by total_ms descending (slowest first)

Task 5 — ProcessPoolExecutor parallelization:
  workers = min(cpu_count-1, 8); chunks of 50 stocks per task
  Module-level _apply_group_impl / _compute_chunk for pickle compat
  Fallback to sequential on ProcessPoolExecutor failure
  CCI All-NaN RuntimeWarning now suppressed within warnings.catch_warnings()

v0.2.3: Auto-dismiss modals and cookie banners after navigation

Sites with aria-modal dialogs (Booking.com, Airbnb) blocked the accessibility
tree to 2-3 elements. Navigate now runs a three-tier dismissal strategy after
page load: (1) known consent SDK selectors (OneTrust, CookieBot, Didomi, etc.),
(2) text/aria-label pattern matching on buttons inside modal containers,
(3) fallback scan of [aria-modal]/[role=dialog] overlays.

Tested: Booking.com 3→177 elements, Airbnb 2→131 elements.
Also syncs Russian docs with v0.2.2 changes (node dist/ commands, --headless flag).

commit.message:
dev: enhance virtual environment management (#261)

* feat: enhance venv management with unswitch and activate features

Three enhancements to virtual environment management:

1. Enhanced venv-clean: Now recognizes when a venv is currently active
   (renamed to .venv) and operates on it correctly. Checks .info.json
   to find the active venv before attempting cleanup.

2. New venv-unswitch: Reverses a venv-switch operation by renaming
   .venv back to .venv-{active} and setting active to null. Provides
   both single component (venv-unswitch) and all components
   (venv-unswitch-all) targets.

3. New activate-venv.sh: Standalone shell script for session-level
   venv activation. Sources activation script directly in current shell
   without persistent filesystem changes. Perfect for quick testing
   or switching between venvs.

All features are fully tested and documented in venv-management.md.

Modified:
  - .claude/rules/venv-management.md: Added documentation for new features
  - scripts/clean_venv.py: Added active venv detection via .info.json
  - Makefile: Added venv-unswitch and venv-unswitch-all targets

Created:
  - scripts/unswitch_venv.py: Implementation of unswitch logic
  - scripts/activate-venv.sh: Standalone activation script

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: repair venv ref after unswitch

* refactor: improve error handling for venv-switch-all, venv-clean-all, venv-unswitch-all

Replace immediate exit on failure with graceful error handling:

1. venv-switch-all: Continues attempting all components even if some
   fail. Provides summary showing succeeded/failed count, lists failed
   components, and suggests how to fix missing venvs.

2. venv-clean-all: Similar graceful error handling with summary of
   cleaned vs failed components.

3. venv-unswitch-all: Treats missing active venvs as a non-failure
   state (components are already independent). Shows informational
   summary and completes successfully.

User Benefits:
  ✓ See which components succeeded and which failed at a glance
  ✓ Get helpful suggestions for fixing failures
  ✓ Don't need to rerun after fixing issues - see full picture
  ✓ Better understanding of system state after operations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: improve venv-create-all and venv-switch-all UX

Two improvements for better user experience:

1. Add MISSING_ONLY flag to venv-create-all
   - make venv-create-all MISSING_ONLY=true GROUP=dev
   - Only creates venvs that don't already exist
   - Shows summary: X created, Y skipped
   - Useful for fixing failed switches without recreating existing venvs

2. Print final state table after venv-switch-all
   - Shows venv-get-active summary after switch operations
   - Users see final state immediately without running separate command
   - Especially helpful for failure scenarios showing partial completion
   - Improved suggestion: suggests MISSING_ONLY=true for creating missing venvs

User Benefits:
  ✓ Faster recovery from failed switches (don't recreate existing venvs)
  ✓ See final state without extra commands
  ✓ Clear visual feedback on what was skipped vs created
  ✓ Better workflow for partial failures

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: move venv-unswitch-all logic to Python script

- Enhanced unswitch_venv.py to support --all flag for unswitching all components
- Implemented graceful error handling for components with no active venv
- Added edge case handling for corrupt state (duplicate .venv and .venv-{id})
- Simplified Makefile venv-unswitch-all target (5 lines -> 1 line)
- Script now tracks succeeded/failed counts and displays summary
- Maintains consistent state tracking via .info.json
- Extracted complex logic into _check_unswitch_result helper function

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: venv-create-all MISSING_ONLY now recognizes active venvs

- Created check_venv_exists.py helper script
- Detects venvs whether they're physical (.venv-{group}) or active (.venv)
- Checks .info.json to determine if active venv matches requested group
- Simplified venv-create-all Makefile logic (removed venv_name/venv_path checks)
- MISSING_ONLY=true now correctly skips active venvs without re-creating them

Test:
  make venv-create-all MISSING_ONLY=true GROUP=dev
  → Now skips all existing dev venvs (0 created, 4 skipped)

Without customer_address for plumbing bookings, the business can't travel to
the customer. If the COLLECT ADDRESS rule is removed from agentBasePrompts,
all on-site bookings become useless.

Adds 2 regression tests:
- COLLECT ADDRESS FOR ON-SITE SERVICES instruction exists with 'MUST ask' mandate
- ALWAYS include customer_address for on-site services guidance in create_booking params

1408 -> 1410 tests total.

Add batch fit visualization generator and all 167 4-panel diagnostic plots

Create comprehensive distribution fit visualizations for all material-technology
pairs to support manual inspection, quality assurance, and manuscript preparation.

New script: diagnostics/generate_all_fit_visualizations.py
- Generates 4-panel plots (histogram+PDF, CDF, Q-Q plot, MC validation)
- Fits lognormal distributions and computes GOF metrics (KS test, RMSE)
- Organizes outputs by sample size bin (n1, n2, n3, n4-5, n6-10, n11-20, n20plus)
- Creates comprehensive index with fit quality assessment

Output: outputs/fitting_sample_size_diagnostics/fit_visualizations/
- 167 PNG files (200 DPI, 39 MB total)
- 143 successful fits (86%)
- 24 failed fits (14%, all n=1 or problematic n=2)
- Indexed by bin with median KS p-values and RMSE

Key findings from visualizations:
- High-n pairs (n≥20): Excellent lognormal fits, narrow uncertainty
- Mid-n pairs (n=5-20): Good fits, moderate uncertainty
- Low-n pairs (n=2-4): Variable fit quality, wide uncertainty
- n=1: Cannot fit (single data point)

Use cases: Manual review, supplementary materials, referee responses,
identifying problematic fits for special handling in uncertainty quantification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add OAuth token refresh tests for tool calling/listing (#8)

* delete plan.md

* Add OAuth token refresh tests for tool calling/listing

Add tests confirming that expired OAuth tokens are refreshed when calling or listing tools:
- Unit tests in oauth.test.ts: Mock refreshAuthorization, verify the happy path refreshes tokens and persists to disk
- Integration tests in manager.test.ts: Verify ServerManager.getClient() triggers refresh for HTTP servers with expired tokens

Bump version to 0.3.4.

commit.message:
Fix pipeline Steps 13-15 — ref code mismatch between intake and build/approve/rollback

Root cause: client_intake table has no 'email' column (only client_email).
Submit route and pipeline test fallback both sent 'email' field, causing
Supabase PGRST204 error — insert silently failed, row never written.
Steps 13-15 queried by ref_code and got 404.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
